metaTitle: Land Registration Cost in Bangladesh 2026: Fee Breakdown & Calculator
title: Land Registration Cost in Bangladesh 2026: Complete Fee Breakdown & Tax Calculator Guide
metaDescription: Detailed guide to land registration fees in Bangladesh for 2026 — stamp duty, registration fee, gain tax, AIT, local government tax, e-Namjari charges, and sub-registry office costs.
keywords: advance income tax land sale Bangladesh
heroIntro: Buying land or property in Bangladesh involves several statutory fees, taxes, and registry expenses. Under the latest Finance Act and Land Registration Rules 2026, the total registration cost typically ranges between 10% to 14% of the deed value, depending on whether the property is located within a City Corporation, Municipality, or Union Parishad. Understanding these exact rates prevents overpayment and protects buyers from fraudulent sub-registry middleman charges. For expert land verification and deed drafting, consult Supreme Court Advocate Advocate Md. Shah Alam.

quickAnswer.heading: ⚡ Quick Summary: Land Registration Fees in Bangladesh 2026
points: Stamp Duty: 1.5% to 3% of the total deed value.
Registration Fee: 1% of the deed value (paid to Sub-Registry Office).
Advance Income Tax (AIT / Gain Tax): 2% to 5% depending on location (City Corp vs Rural).
Local Government Tax: 2% to 3% (Paurashava / City Corporation).
E-Namjari / Mutation Fee: BDT 1,170 fixed online statutory fee.

sections.h2: Overview of Land Registration Cost Components in 2026
sections.content:
When registering a land purchase deed (Saf Kabala / সাফ কবালা দলিল) in Bangladesh, the registration cost is not a single lump sum. It comprises multiple Government duties, local municipality taxes, and statutory processing fees payable under the Registration Act 1908 and the Stamp Act 1899.

The primary components include:

  • Stamp Duty (স্ট্যাম্প ডিউটি): Revenue stamp fee collected by the National Board of Revenue (NBR).
  • Registration Fee (রেজিস্ট্রেশন ফি): Statutory fee for registering the document in the Sub-Registry Office.
  • Local Government Tax (স্থানীয় সরকার কর): Tax payable to City Corporations, Municipalities (Paurashava), or Zilla Parishads.
  • Capital Gain Tax / Advance Income Tax (উৎস কর / এআইটি): Tax levied on the seller's property transaction under Income Tax Act.
  • E-Namjari Fee (ই-নামজারি ফি): Online mutation fee payable to the AC Land Office.

Consulting an experienced Land Lawyer in Dhaka before deed registration ensures all tax chalans and pay orders are accurately calculated.

sections.h2: Detailed Percentage Breakdown of Registry Fees
sections.content:
Below is the standard statutory fee table for land deed registration in Bangladesh for 2026:

Fee Head Percentage / Rate Payable To
Stamp Duty 1.5% to 3.0% of Deed Value NBR (Treasury Chalan)
Registration Fee 1.0% of Deed Value Sub-Registry Office
Local Govt Tax 2.0% (City Corp) / 3.0% (Paurashava) Local Government Body
Gain Tax (AIT) 2.0% to 5.0% (Location Dependent) Tax Zone / NBR
E-Namjari Fee BDT 1,170 (Fixed) Land Ministry Portal

sections.h2: Location-Based Tax Rate Variations (Dhaka vs Outside)
sections.content:
The total registration cost varies significantly depending on the geographical location of the property:

  • Dhaka, Chittagong, Gazipur City Corporation Areas: Highest AIT rates apply (up to 5% or fixed rate per Katha depending on commercial/residential zone like Gulshan, Banani, Motijheel, Uttara).
  • Other City Corporations & District Municipalities: Moderate AIT rates (3% to 4%) and 2% Local Govt Tax.
  • Rural / Union Parishad Areas: Lowest AIT rate (2%) and 1% Zilla Parishad Tax. Total registration cost is lowest in rural areas (approx 8.5% to 10%).

sections.h2: Capital Gain Tax & Advance Income Tax (AIT) Rules
sections.content:
Under the Income Tax Act 2023 and updated Finance Rules, Gain Tax (Source Tax under Section 53H) is legally the seller's responsibility, though in commercial practice buyers often negotiate this in the total contract value.

Key Gain Tax rules include:

  • Property sold within 5 years of acquisition may attract higher tax rates.
  • Commercial land in prime Dhaka hubs (Motijheel, Kawran Bazar, Dhanmondi, Uttara) has fixed per-katha tax rates set by the National Board of Revenue.
  • Exemption applies to specific inherited properties or gift transfers between primary family members under Heba Bil Ewaz.

After registering the deed, property ownership is incomplete without E-Namjari (ই-নামজারি) at the Assistant Commissioner (Land) Office. The Government of Bangladesh has automated this via land.gov.bd.

Statutory Breakdown of the BDT 1,170 Mutation Fee:

  • Application Fee: BDT 70
  • Notice Issue Fee: BDT 50
  • Khatian Fee: BDT 100
  • Mutation Fee (DCR Fee): BDT 950
  • Total Official Charge: BDT 1,170

Never pay excessive illegal charges for online mutation. If an AC Land office rejects or delays your application, consult a qualified lawyer to file a Miss Case or Appeal.

sections.h2: Sub-Registry Office Additional Official Fees & Cartridge Paper
sections.content:
Besides percentage fees, minor fixed statutory charges apply during deed presentation:

  • E-Fee: BDT 100 per deed.
  • N-Fee (Search Fee): BDT 20 to BDT 100 per volume book search.
  • Cartridge Paper & Writing Charge: BDT 300 to BDT 500.
  • Deed Writer (দলিল লেখক) License Commission: Governed by official scale or mutual agreement.

sections.h2: How to Calculate Total Cost: Practical Examples
sections.content:
Example Scenario: Purchasing a Flat / Land in Uttara, Dhaka valued at BDT 1,00,00,000 (1 Crore):

  • Stamp Duty (1.5%): BDT 1,50,000
  • Registration Fee (1%): BDT 1,00,000
  • Local Govt Tax (2%): BDT 2,00,000
  • Gain Tax / AIT (approx 4%): BDT 4,00,000
  • Misc Stamps & Charges: BDT 5,000
  • Estimated Total Registration Cost: BDT 8,55,000 (approx 8.55%)

sections.h2: Crucial Legal Checklist Before Registering Land in Dhaka
sections.content:
Before depositing bank pay orders for land registration, ensure your advocate conducts complete due diligence:

  1. Verify CS, SA, RS, and BS Khatians at the District Record Room.
  2. Check if the land is listed as Khas (Government), Vested Property, or Wetlands.
  3. Verify original deed title flow (Via Deed / বায়া দলিল) for 25 years.
  4. Verify seller's online E-Namjari and latest Land Development Tax (Khajna) receipt.
  5. Obtain non-encumbrance certificate (NEC) from the Sub-Registry office.

Advocate Md. Shah Alam — Supreme Court Advocate with 20+ years experience — provides complete land title verification, deed drafting, and sub-registry representation in Uttara and across Dhaka.

faqs.question: Who is legally responsible for paying land registration fees in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: By law and custom, the buyer pays the Stamp Duty, Registration Fee, and Local Government Tax. The seller is responsible for the Capital Gain Tax (AIT), though contract terms may specify otherwise.

faqs.question: What happens if I register land at a lower deed value than actual market value?
faqs.answer: Under-invoicing to evade tax is illegal. Sub-registrars verify official government minimum land rates (Mouza Rate). Registration below the Mouza Rate will be rejected or impounded for penalty.

faqs.question: How long after deed registration must I complete E-Namjari?
faqs.answer: E-Namjari should be filed immediately after receiving the deed copy. Delaying mutation leaves your property vulnerable to illegal sale or double registration by fraudsters.

faqs.question: Can non-resident Bangladeshis (NRBs) register land via Power of Attorney?
faqs.answer: Yes. NRBs can execute an Irrevocable Power of Attorney authenticated by the Bangladesh Embassy/High Commission abroad, attested by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) in Dhaka.
